description: CloudTrail is not configured for multi-region logging, leaving API activity in other regions unmonitored.
impact: Security events in regions without CloudTrail coverage will go undetected, creating blind spots in incident response and forensic investigations.
fix_suggestion: Enable multi-region trail in CloudTrail configuration to capture API activity across all AWS regions.

description: EC2 instance has a public IP address assigned, making it directly reachable from the internet without going through a load balancer or proxy.
impact: Directly exposed instances are vulnerable to port scanning, exploitation of running services, and DDoS attacks.
fix_suggestion: Remove the public IP assignment and place the instance behind a load balancer or NAT gateway. Use VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

description: EBS volumes attached to this EC2 instance are not encrypted, leaving data-at-rest unprotected.
impact: If the EBS volume is snapshotted, shared, or the underlying hardware is decommissioned, sensitive data may be exposed.
fix_suggestion: Enable EBS encryption by default in the account settings and encrypt existing volumes by creating encrypted snapshots and restoring from them.

description: IAM user account does not have Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled, relying solely on password-based authentication.
impact: Compromised credentials can be exploited without any second-factor challenge, enabling full account takeover and lateral movement.
fix_suggestion: Enable virtual MFA or hardware MFA device for the IAM user. Enforce MFA via IAM policies for sensitive API operations.

description: IAM user does not have Multi-Factor Authentication enabled, relying solely on a single authentication factor.
impact: Single-factor authentication makes the account susceptible to credential theft, phishing, and brute-force attacks.
fix_suggestion: Enable MFA for the IAM user using a virtual MFA device or hardware token. Enforce MFA through IAM policy conditions.

description: S3 bucket does not have the BlockPublicAcls setting enabled in its Public Access Block configuration, allowing public ACLs to be applied to bucket objects.
impact: Unauthorized users could gain read or write access to sensitive bucket data through public ACLs, leading to potential data breaches.
fix_suggestion: Enable all four Public Access Block settings (BlockPublicAcls, IgnorePublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, RestrictPublicBuckets) on the S3 bucket.

description: Server-side encryption is not enabled on the S3 bucket, leaving stored objects unprotected at rest.
impact: Data stored in the bucket is vulnerable to unauthorized access if physical storage media is compromised or access controls are bypassed.
fix_suggestion: Enable default server-side encryption on the S3 bucket using SSE-S3 (AES-256) or SSE-KMS with a customer-managed key.

description: Object versioning is not enabled on the S3 bucket, preventing recovery of accidentally overwritten or deleted objects.
impact: Accidental or malicious deletion of objects cannot be recovered, increasing risk of data loss during operational errors or ransomware attacks.
fix_suggestion: Enable versioning on the S3 bucket and configure lifecycle rules to manage version retention costs.

description: Server-side encryption is not enabled on the storage resource, leaving data at rest unprotected.
impact: Unencrypted data may be exposed if access controls are bypassed or storage media is compromised.
fix_suggestion: Enable server-side encryption using the cloud provider native encryption service or customer-managed keys.

description: Object versioning is not enabled on the S3 bucket, preventing recovery of accidentally overwritten or deleted objects.
impact: Accidental or malicious deletion of objects cannot be recovered, increasing risk of data loss during operational errors or ransomware attacks.
fix_suggestion: Enable versioning on the S3 bucket and configure lifecycle rules to manage version retention costs.

description: Security group has inbound rules allowing traffic from 0.0.0.0/0, permitting unrestricted access from any IP address on the internet.
impact: All associated resources are directly reachable from the internet, enabling reconnaissance, brute-force attacks, and exploitation of exposed services.
fix_suggestion: Restrict inbound rules to specific trusted CIDR blocks or security group references instead of allowing 0.0.0.0/0.

description: Security group allows inbound SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0, exposing the SSH service to the entire internet.
impact: Attackers can attempt brute-force or credential-stuffing attacks against SSH, potentially gaining full shell access to the instance.
fix_suggestion: Restrict SSH access to a bastion host or specific trusted IP ranges. Consider using AWS Systems Manager Session Manager as a zero-port alternative.

description: Security group permits inbound SSH (port 22) access from 0.0.0.0/0, exposing any associated instance to internet-facing SSH attacks.
impact: Unrestricted SSH access enables brute-force login attempts and exploitation of SSH vulnerabilities, potentially leading to full system compromise.
fix_suggestion: Restrict the SSH inbound rule to specific trusted IP addresses or CIDR blocks and consider using a bastion host architecture.
